visualizerParams.title: Square Root Digital Expansion
visualizerParams.description: Calculate the total sum of the first 100 decimal digits for irrational square roots of numbers 1-100.
visualizerParams.ghostCode:
from decimal import Decimal, getcontext

def solution() -> int:
    """
    Returns the total sum of the first 100 digits for irrational square roots up to 100.
    """
    # Set precision to slightly more than 100 to avoid rounding errors
    getcontext().prec = 110
    total_sum = 0
    
    for n in range(1, 101):
        sqrt_n = Decimal(n).sqrt()
        
        # Check if root is irrational (not an integer)
        if sqrt_n % 1 != 0:
            # Get digits string, remove decimal point
            digit_str = str(sqrt_n).replace('.', '')[:100]
            total_sum += sum(int(d) for d in digit_str)
            
    return total_sum


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(f"Total digit sum: {solution()}")
visualizerParams.finalCode:
from decimal import Decimal, getcontext

def solution() -> int:
    """
    Returns the total sum of the first 100 digits for irrational square roots up to 100.
    """
    # Set precision to slightly more than 100 to avoid rounding errors
    getcontext().prec = 110
    total_sum = 0
    
    for n in range(1, 101):
        sqrt_n = Decimal(n).sqrt()
        
        # Check if root is irrational (not an integer)
        if sqrt_n % 1 != 0:
            # Get digits string, remove decimal point
            digit_str = str(sqrt_n).replace('.', '')[:100]
            total_sum += sum(int(d) for d in digit_str)
            
    return total_sum


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(f"Total digit sum: {solution()}")
